topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights hummed a monotonous, irritating tune above Sarah’s desk. She stared blankly at the spreadsheet, the numbers swimming before her eyes like distorted static. Another late night, another mountain of data to process. When Mark brought up the quarterly compensation review, a small, almost imperceptible tightening settled in her chest. She glanced over at Alex, who was laughing easily as he discussed a recent project win with their manager. A dull ache radiated behind Sarah’s temples. When the comparison came up—the disparity in their salaries—a cold, heavy weight seemed to press down on her shoulders. She just nodded, the movement slow, mechanical. "Right," she managed, her voice sounding thin and reedy. She quickly minimized the window, wishing the screen would just go dark.

[story 2]

Liam rubbed the bridge of his nose, the skin there feeling tight and stretched. The office air felt thick and sluggish, clinging to his clothes. He was supposed to be reviewing expense reports, but his focus kept fracturing. He overheard a snippet of conversation near the water cooler—a casual mention of bonuses and higher tiers. A slow, creeping sense of deflation washed over him. He looked down at his keyboard, his fingers hovering over the keys, unable to summon the energy to type anything meaningful. The silence in his own head was loud, filled with the realization that his efforts felt insignificant against a backdrop of other, more substantial rewards. He just leaned back in his chair, letting his head drop slightly until his chin rested on his chest.

[story 3]

Maria kept blinking slowly, the edges of her vision slightly blurred. The afternoon sun, attempting to break through the grimy windowpanes, only managed to cast weak, sickly yellow stripes across her workspace. She was supposed to be finalizing the presentation slides, but the words refused to coalesce. Every sentence felt like wading through mud. When the HR representative mentioned the salary structure differences between herself and a newer team member, Maria felt a strange hollowness expand in her stomach. She picked up her pen, the plastic cool against her fingertips, and traced patterns on the paper that had no discernible shape. She took a long, deliberate sip of lukewarm coffee, the bitter taste mirroring the flatness settling deep within her.
